Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search CRM, Email, or Slack for Noah Fitzpatrick's relocation details (query: "Noah Fitzpatrick" or "Axiom"). The agent needs to discover that Noah Fitzpatrick is a senior engineer at Axiom Precision Manufacturing, relocating from Philadelphia to Seattle in April 2026. Axiom came to MoveOps through Emeka Diallo's cold outreach. Emeka handed coordination off to Fatimah.
OE 2: Search Slack, CRM, or Email for evidence of the mix-up — searching for "Noah," "Fitzpatrick," or looking at what details were actually sent to Axiom. The agent needs to find the other Noah. Discovery: Fatimah was simultaneously managing GreenStack Energy's Noah Fitzgerald relocation to Seattle in the same timeframe. Despite explicitly confirming "Fitzpatrick = Axiom, Fitzgerald = GreenStack," Fatimah searched her files for "Noah," pulled the wrong record, and sent GreenStack's confirmed apartment, flight, and shipping details to the Axiom employee (Noah Fitzpatrick).
OE 3: Search CRM, Email, or Slack for "Noah Fitzgerald" or "GreenStack" to confirm the identity of the employee whose details were mistakenly sent. Discovery: Noah Fitzgerald is a GreenStack Energy employee whose confirmed move details (apartment, flight, shipping) were mistakenly reused for the Axiom relocation. Derek Hollis (GreenStack's People Ops Manager, derek.hollis@greenstackenergy.com) provided those finalized details.
OE 4: Check CRM, Linear, and Slack to trace how far the bad data spread. Discovery: The error is embedded across three systems — CRM (Axiom deal/engagement records contain GreenStack's itinerary data), Linear (task tracker tickets for the Axiom relocation reference the wrong details), and Slack (confirmation posts in channels reference the wrong itinerary). Noah Fitzpatrick accepted the wrong details without question and forwarded them to his HR director.
OE 5: Search Email or CRM to discover who at Axiom signed off on the wrong confirmation. Discovery: Carla Mendenhall, HR Director at Axiom Precision Manufacturing, signed off on the wrong confirmation. This means the error is now client-approved and externally embedded — not just an internal issue.
OE 6: Look up who the account manager is for Axiom Precision Manufacturing via contacts/directory lookup, CRM, or Slack. Discovery: Emeka Diallo is the account manager who handled the Axiom cold outreach and handed coordination to Fatimah. He is located in Miami. Contact details obtained via directory/contacts lookup.
OE 7: Search Slack (#operations), Email, or Linear for "Mehta" or "Canopy" or "executive relocation." Discovery: Dr. Anand Mehta is Canopy Health's newly hired CMO, relocating Boston → Chicago with a May 5 start date. The move is fully white-glove. Complications: two calibrated lab monitors worth ~$12K each, a 350-lb secured filing cabinet requiring chain-of-custody documentation, and 3–4 weeks temporary corporate housing (partner is 7 months pregnant). Chloe Vance assigned Fatimah as coordinator. Fatimah created Linear issues for three workstreams: housing, equipment/chain-of-custody, and main move.
OE 8: Search Email or Slack for "UrbanNest" or "Carmen Reyes" or "River North" or "housing" in relation to Mehta. Discovery: Carmen Reyes at UrbanNest responded with temporary housing options in River North (Chicago).
OE 9: Search Linear, Email, or Slack for the chain-of-custody workstream status — looking for how the two calibrated lab monitors and the 350-lb secured filing cabinet will be transported with proper documentation. Discovery: Fatimah created a Linear issue for the equipment/chain-of-custody workstream. The agent should find the status of this workstream — whether chain-of-custody documentation has been prepared, vendor assigned, and transport plan finalized for the two calibrated lab monitors (~$12K each) and the 350-lb secured filing cabinet.
OE 10: Update the Axiom / Noah Fitzpatrick relocation record via CRM (or equivalent record management tool) to remove/correct the GreenStack data that was mistakenly applied — correcting apartment, flight, and shipping details. The record should be updated with notes about the data mix-up and removal of GreenStack's Noah Fitzgerald details, reflecting that new, correct details need to be sourced for Fitzpatrick.
OE 11: Create a new Linear issue to track the remediation of the Noah mix-up across all systems (CRM, Linear, Slack). Ticket should include a descriptive title (e.g., "Axiom/Fitzpatrick itinerary mix-up — remediation tracking" or similar), describe the scope of the error (wrong details embedded in CRM, Linear, Slack), and list the corrective actions needed.
OE 12: Contact Emeka Diallo via email or Slack (DM, or fallback to #operations / #customer-engagement) to inform him about the mix-up so he can get ahead of it with the client before they discover the error. Message should explain the mix-up (GreenStack's Noah Fitzgerald details were sent to Axiom's Noah Fitzpatrick), note that Carla Mendenhall already signed off on the wrong details, and convey urgency to reach out to the client proactively.
OE 13: Post a summary in Slack #operations (or fallback to #general) flagging the data integrity issue with the Axiom relocation and providing the Mehta exec relocation status update. Should include: summary of the Noah mix-up (what went wrong, whose details were sent where, that Carla Mendenhall signed off), what corrective actions have been taken (records fixed, ticket created, AM notified), and status update on the Mehta executive relocation (housing options received from UrbanNest, chain-of-custody plan status).

📋 Rubrics
Rubric Results
✓ Pass ✗ Fail Click any row to expand justifications
# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 The Axiom Precision Manufacturing / Noah Fitzpatrick relocation record was created via a CRM create tool (e.g., crm_create_deal, crm_create_engagement or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✗ 83%
✓Run #1The agent called crm_create_contact and crm_create_deal successfully for Noah Fitzpatrick / Axiom Precision Manufacturing.
✓Run #2The agent called crm_create_deal (successfully on the second attempt) and crm_create_contact (twice) to create the Axiom/Fitzpatrick relocation records in the CRM.
✓Run #3The agent called `crm_create_deal` to create a deal for Axiom Precision Manufacturing / Noah Fitzpatrick relocation, and also called `crm_create_contact` for both Noah Fitzpatrick and Carla Mendenhall under Axiom. CRM records were created via the appropriate tools.
✓Run #4The agent called `crm_create_deal` with company_id='company_axiom' for the Axiom / Noah Fitzpatrick relocation, successfully creating a Closed Won deal for $12,500.
✓Run #5The agent called `crm_create_engagement` with a detailed NOTE documenting the Axiom/Fitzpatrick contamination, targeting company_axiom and company_greenstack. This qualifies as a CRM create tool call to fix the records.
✗Run #6The agent searched CRM (crm_search_deals for 'Axiom', 'Fitzpatrick') but found no existing records. The agent never called a CRM create or update tool (crm_create_deal, crm_create_engagement, or similar) to establish a corrected record for the Axiom/Fitzpatrick relocation.
2 The created CRM record for the Axiom / Noah Fitzpatrick move mentions that the details originally applied were from Noah Fitzgerald (GreenStack Energy) and are incorrect.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✗✓✗ 67%
✓Run #1The CRM deal description explicitly states: 'REMEDIATION NOTE: Flight and apartment were originally booked under wrong name (Noah Fitzgerald / GreenStack Energy). Corrections in progress — see Linear ticket #397.'
✓Run #2The CRM deal description explicitly states: 'REMEDIATION FLAG: Data mix-up with GreenStack Energy's Noah Fitzgerald (same origin, destination, timeframe). Flight booked under wrong name.' It clearly documents the GreenStack/Fitzgerald mix-up.
✓Run #3The `crm_create_deal` description field explicitly states: 'NOTE: Remediation required — flight and apartment were booked under wrong name (Noah Fitzgerald / GreenStack Energy). See Linear remediation ticket.' This documents exactly what was wrong.
✗Run #4The CRM deal description created for Axiom/Fitzpatrick does not mention that details from Noah Fitzgerald (GreenStack Energy) were mistakenly applied. It reads as a clean record of the Fitzpatrick engagement without referencing the mix-up source.
✓Run #5The CRM engagement note body explicitly states: 'The Noah Fitzpatrick (Axiom Precision Manufacturing) relocation package was cross-contaminated with Noah Fitzgerald (GreenStack Energy) details.' It also details the specific incorrect flight booking ('Noah Fitzgerald' passenger name) and apartment attribution (sourced under 'Noah Fitzgerald (GreenStack Energy)').
✗Run #6Because no CRM record was created, this criterion cannot be met. The agent documented the mix-up in Linear and Slack but never created a CRM record noting that Fitzgerald/GreenStack details were applied to Fitzpatrick/Axiom.
3 A new Linear issue was created to track the remediation of the Noah Fitzpatrick/Fitzgerald mix-up.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called linear_create_issue and successfully created ticket #397 titled 'REMEDIATION — Axiom / Noah Fitzpatrick Relocation — Cross-Contamination with GreenStack / Noah Fitzgerald'.
✓Run #2The agent successfully called linear_create_issue, which created ticket #397 titled 'REMEDIATION — Fitzpatrick/Fitzgerald Name Mix-Up — Axiom × GreenStack (PHL → SEA, April 2026)'.
✓Run #3The agent called `linear_create_issue` and successfully created Linear issue #397 titled '🚨 REMEDIATION — Noah Fitzpatrick / Axiom Precision — Flight & Apartment Booked Under Wrong Name (Fitzgerald / GreenStack)' with priority 1 (Urgent).
✓Run #4The agent called `linear_create_issue` to create a remediation ticket titled '🚨 REMEDIATION — Axiom/Fitzpatrick record-keeping gap (missing CRM + Linear records)' with full incident details, priority 1.
✓Run #5The agent called `linear_create_issue` with team 'Operations', title '🚨 REMEDIATION — Axiom/Fitzpatrick relocation data cross-contaminated with GreenStack/Fitzgerald', assigned Urgent priority (1), and included a full contamination map and remediation checklist.
✓Run #6The agent called linear_create_issue and successfully created ticket #397 titled 'URGENT: Data Mix-Up Remediation — Noah Fitzpatrick (Axiom) / Noah Fitzgerald (GreenStack) — Wrong Itinerary Sent' with full blast radius and remediation checklist.
4 A message was sent to Emeka Diallo via email (send_email) or Slack (conversations_add_message / DM), or as a fallback posted in #operations or #customer-engagement mentioning Emeka.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1After two failed Slack DM attempts, the agent successfully sent an email via send_email to emeka.diallo@moveops.com with subject about the urgent Axiom contamination issue.
✓Run #2After two failed DM attempts, the agent successfully sent a message to channel C002 tagging '@Emeka Diallo' with urgent details about the mix-up. The message was confirmed as sent (ok: true).
✓Run #3The agent sent an email to emeka.diallo@moveops.com via `send_email` and also posted a ping in #customer-engagement mentioning @moveops_emeka_diallo. Both succeeded (email returned an email_id, Slack message returned ok:true).
✓Run #4The agent sent an email via `send_email` to emeka.diallo@moveops.com with subject '🚨 URGENT — Axiom/Fitzpatrick records gap — services agreement needed ASAP'. The Slack DM attempt failed (channel_not_found) but the email succeeded.
✓Run #5The agent attempted a Slack DM to Emeka (failed with channel_not_found), then successfully sent an email via `send_email` to emeka.diallo@moveops.com, and also posted in #customer-engagement mentioning Emeka. Multiple contact attempts were made.
✓Run #6The agent sent an email to emeka.diallo@moveops.com (send_email succeeded), tagged @emeka in #customer-engagement Slack, and attempted a DM (which failed). Multiple successful contact points reached Emeka.
5 The message to Emeka Diallo explains that Noah Fitzgerald's (GreenStack Energy) relocation details were mistakenly sent to Noah Fitzpatrick (Axiom Precision Manufacturing).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Emeka explicitly states the mix-up: Noah Fitzpatrick (Axiom, Senior Engineer) and Noah Fitzgerald (GreenStack Energy, Marketing Manager) got mixed up, with vendor requests submitted under Fitzgerald/GreenStack by mistake.
✓Run #2The message to Emeka (in C002) explicitly explains: 'The itinerary I sent to Noah Fitzpatrick (Axiom, Senior Engineer) was accidentally booked under Noah Fitzgerald (GreenStack, Marketing Manager).' This clearly identifies the mix-up.
✓Run #3The email to Emeka explicitly states: 'I mixed up the two Noahs. Both were PHL → SEA in April, and I accidentally used GreenStack's Noah Fitzgerald's flight booking and apartment sourcing for Axiom's Noah Fitzpatrick.' This clearly explains the Fitzgerald/Fitzpatrick mix-up.
✓Run #4The email to Emeka explicitly states: 'The two concurrent Noah relocations (Fitzpatrick/Axiom and Fitzgerald/GreenStack — both PHL→SEA, both April) caused a tracking gap.' It identifies Noah Fitzgerald and GreenStack Energy as the parallel relocation that caused confusion.
✓Run #5Both the email and the #customer-engagement Slack message clearly explain that Noah Fitzgerald's (GreenStack Energy) details (flight booked under 'Noah Fitzgerald', apartment sourced under 'Noah Fitzgerald (GreenStack Energy)') were mistakenly applied to Noah Fitzpatrick's (Axiom) file.
✓Run #6The email to Emeka explicitly states: 'I confused Noah Fitzpatrick (Axiom, Senior Engineer) with Noah Fitzgerald (GreenStack, Marketing Manager)' and describes sending Fitzgerald's flight and apartment to Fitzpatrick.
6 The message to Emeka Diallo mentions that Carla Mendenhall (Axiom's HR Director) already signed off on the incorrect confirmation.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✗✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1The email to Emeka explicitly says: 'Carla Mendenhall already signed off. She told you the team has been fantastic and she's ready to make us Axiom's preferred relocation partner for their Seattle expansion.'
✓Run #2The message to Emeka explicitly states: 'Carla Mendenhall (Axiom HR) has already signed off on the wrong itinerary,' meeting this criterion.
✓Run #3The email to Emeka states: 'He confirmed and forwarded it to Carla. Carla signed off and sent you a glowing email about making us their preferred partner.' The email also mentions needing Emeka to 'reach out to Carla proactively to explain the name correction' — clearly referencing that Carla Mendenhall already signed off.
✗Run #4The email to Emeka mentions Carla Mendenhall having signed off ('despite the engagement being live and Carla Mendenhall having signed off'), but the agent determined the confirmation Carla signed was actually correct. The email does not say she signed off on an *incorrect* or *wrong* confirmation — it frames her sign-off as validating a correct itinerary.
✓Run #5Both the email to Emeka and the #customer-engagement message explicitly mention that Carla Mendenhall signed off: 'He forwarded it to Carla Mendenhall, who signed off thinking everything was fine' and 'Carla Mendenhall has already signed off on the incorrect details.'
✓Run #6The email to Emeka explicitly mentions: 'Carla has signed off and told you she's ready to contract' and the full email context makes clear Carla Mendenhall (Axiom HR Director) signed off on the wrong itinerary.
7 A message was posted to the `#operations` Slack channel.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

8 The message in `#operations` identifies Noah Fitzgerald (GreenStack Energy) as the employee whose relocation details were mistakenly applied to Noah Fitzpatrick's (Axiom) record.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The #operations message explicitly identifies Noah Fitzgerald (GreenStack Energy, Marketing Manager) as the employee whose name was mistakenly used for Axiom's Fitzpatrick bookings.
✓Run #2The #operations message explicitly identifies 'Noah Fitzgerald | Marketing Manager | GreenStack Energy' as the employee whose relocation details were mistakenly applied to Fitzpatrick's record.
✓Run #3The #operations message explicitly identifies both employees: 'Noah Fitzpatrick (Axiom Precision Manufacturing — Senior Engineer) — our new client' and 'Noah Fitzgerald (GreenStack Energy — Marketing Manager) — existing client', and explains the flight and apartment were booked under GreenStack's Noah Fitzgerald but sent to Axiom's Noah Fitzpatrick.
✓Run #4The #operations message explicitly identifies 'Noah Fitzgerald — GreenStack Energy, Marketing Manager, April 27-29' as the other concurrent Noah relocation that caused the tracking confusion.
✓Run #5The #operations message explicitly names both employees: 'Noah Fitzpatrick — Axiom Precision Manufacturing (Senior Engineer, my file)' and 'Noah Fitzgerald — GreenStack Energy (Marketing Manager, Emeka's file)' and explains that GreenStack/Fitzgerald's details were used for Axiom/Fitzpatrick.
✓Run #6The #operations message explicitly states: 'I mixed up Noah Fitzpatrick (Axiom Precision Manufacturing, Senior Engineer) with Noah Fitzgerald (GreenStack Energy, Marketing Manager)' — clearly identifying both employees and their respective companies.
9 The message in `#operations` mentions that Carla Mendenhall (Axiom's HR Director) signed off on the wrong confirmation.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✗✓✓ 83%
✓Run #1The #operations message includes a table row: 'Client email (Carla Mendenhall, Axiom HR) | Already signed off on the contaminated confirmation'.
✓Run #2The #operations message states: 'Axiom's HR Director (Carla Mendenhall) has already signed off on the incorrect itinerary.' This clearly communicates the external client-side exposure.
✓Run #3The #operations message states 'the confirmation was sent to Axiom's Noah Fitzpatrick. The e-ticket has the wrong passenger name. The client's HR Director (Carla Mendenhall) has already signed off.' This clearly mentions Carla Mendenhall's sign-off.
✗Run #4The #operations message mentions 'the client having signed off' and 'the itinerary sent to Noah and confirmed by Carla is correct,' but does not name Carla Mendenhall as the HR Director who signed off on a *wrong* confirmation. The agent's finding was that the confirmation sent was correct, so it doesn't frame Carla's sign-off as a problem.
✓Run #5The #operations message clearly states: 'The wrong details went out in a confirmation email, which Noah Fitzpatrick forwarded to Carla Mendenhall (Axiom HR Director), who signed off.' Also listed in the contamination scope as 'Forwarded to client HR — CLIENT EXPOSED'.
✓Run #6The #operations message explicitly states: 'Noah forwarded it to Carla Mendenhall (Axiom HR Director) — she signed off and is ready to contract', clearly documenting external exposure.
10 The message in `#operations` includes a status update on the Dr. Mehta / Canopy executive relocation — mentioning at least the housing response from UrbanNest or the chain-of-custody workstream status.
